Field.Label: Pick the device whose power draw signals when this appliance is running (e.g. the Shelly that meters the washing machine). Defaults below suit a washing machine; tweak for other appliances.
Power state name:
Field.Description: State key on the source device that reports watts. Shelly plugin uses "powerWatts".

Field.Label: Energy state name (optional):
Field.Description: State key on the source device that reports a running kWh counter. ShellyDirect uses "energyKwhToday". Leave blank if the meter has no energy counter — the plugin will simply skip the per-cycle kWh measurement.

Field.Label: Rate variable (optional):
Field.Description: Name of an Indigo variable holding your electricity import rate in pence per kWh (e.g. "tracker_rate_today"). When set, every finished cycle gets a cost: cycle kWh times the rate at cycle end. Leave blank to skip costing.

Field.Label: Run threshold (W):
Field.Description: Power at or above this value means the appliance is drawing real power (running).

Field.Label: Idle threshold (W):
Field.Description: Power below this value means the appliance is idle (cycle may have ended).

Field.Label: End-of-cycle debounce (min):
Field.Description: How long power must stay below the idle threshold before the cycle is declared ended. Avoids false ends during mid-cycle quiet phases. Set to 0 to disable debounce (cycle ends on first sub-idle reading — only safe for appliances whose final phase never dips below idleThresholdWatts before fully stopping).

Field.Label: Ignore cycles shorter than (min):
Field.Description: Discard a completed cycle shorter than this, without recording it or sending any notification. Use it to stop a brief standby blip being reported as a wash. 0 disables the check.

Field.Label: Ignore cycles peaking below (W):
Field.Description: Discard a completed cycle whose highest reading never reached this, without recording it or sending any notification. Set it well above standby but below the appliance's real running draw. 0 disables the check.

Field.Label: Door-ready delay (min):
Field.Description: Fires the doorReady event this long after the power actually stopped - NOT after the debounce window above. Set it to the appliance's door-lock release delay. If it is shorter than the debounce, the alert goes out on the same tick the cycle is declared ended.

Field.Label: Socket-reminder delay (min):
Field.Description: Fires the socketReminder event this long after cycle end if no new cycle has started. Use this to prompt yourself to switch off a manual wall socket.

Field.Label: Pushover notifications (sent directly by this plugin in addition to firing the matching Indigo event):
Notify on cycle start:
Field.Description: Send a Pushover when the appliance begins drawing power. Off by default — usually only useful when calibrating.

Field.Label: Per-device Pushover titles (leave blank for the appliance-agnostic default shown):
Cycle-started title:
Field.Description: Default if blank: "Cycle started". Examples: "Wash started", "Dryer started", "Dishwasher started".

Field.Label: Door-ready title:
Field.Description: Default if blank: "Cycle done". Examples: "Wash done", "Dryer done", "Dishwasher done".

Field.Label: Pushover sound:
Field.Description: "vibrate" for silent buzz (default), "pushover" for the standard tone, or any Pushover API sound name.

Field.Label: Pushover user token (optional):
Field.Description: Leave blank to use the default user configured in the Pushover plugin.

Field.Label: Also notify (extra Pushover users):
Field.Description: Comma-separated additional Pushover user keys (or a delivery-group key) to send a copy to, on top of the primary recipient above — e.g. a partner who has their own Pushover account. Each gets the same alert. Leave blank to notify only the primary recipient.

Field.Label: Email notifications (sent in addition to Pushover, for the same events ticked above):
Send email alerts:
Field.Description: Untick to silence email without clearing the recipients below — handy for keeping addresses on file as a dormant fallback while Pushover does the live notifying (so people aren't pinged twice for one event).

Field.Label: Email recipients (optional):
Field.Description: Comma-separated email addresses to notify in addition to Pushover (e.g. "jane@example.com, sam@example.com"). Sent via the Email+ plugin's first SMTP server. Leave blank to send Pushover only.
